3) It was influenced by natural phenomena.

question: INSECTICIDES WERE USED IN THE FOREST RESERVOIRS TO BRING YELLOW FEVER UNDER CONTROL

RESEARCH SHOWED THAT SEVERAL SYNTHETIC COMPOUNDS WERE MORE EFFECTIVE THAN QUININE.
ОТВЕТЬТЕ НА ВОПРОС: WHY DID THE WORLD HEALTH ORGANISATION SPONSOR THE WORLD-WIDE CAMPAIGN OF BRINGING MALARIA UNDER CONTROL?
options: 1) Because a new and highly efficient insecticide DDT appeared.
2) Because malaria was widely spread in Africa.
3) Because it wanted to help reduce the disease among Allied troops in Africa.
4) Because the standards of public health were rising.
correct: 1) Because a new and highly efficient insecticide DDT appeared.

question: ОПРЕДЕЛИТЕ ОСНОВНУЮ ИДЕЮ ТЕКСТА
options: 1) Quinine lost its effect on malaria in the first half of the 20th century.
2) The major diseases of the tropics are malaria, yellow fever, and leprosy.

4) Chaulmoogra oils and their derivatives were helpful for treating leprosy until the 1940s. Text 3 PLASTIC SURGERY
correct: 3) Insecticides did much to bring the three of the major diseases of the tropics under control.

question: AN OPERATING MICROSCOPE HELPS THE SURGEON TO REJOIN SMALL BLOOD VESSELS AND OTHER MINUTE STRUCTURES.
PLASTIC SURGERY IS DIRECTED AT CORRECTING SUCH ABNORMALITIES AS CLEFT LIP AND CLEFT PALATE, AND TUMOURS.

ELECTION MICROSCOPY ENABLED OBSERVERS TO PEER MORE DEEPLY INTO THE STRUCTURES OF THE CELL.
COSMETIC SURGERY IS PERFORMED TO IMPROVE APPEARANCE IN OTHERWISE HEALTHY PEOPLE.
MODERN TECHNIQUES OF PLASTIC SURGERY WERE AIMED AT REPAIRING THE WOUNDS AND DISFIGUREMENTS OF MILITARY MEN.
ОТВЕТЬТЕ НА ВОПРОС: WHAT DOES PLASTIC SURGERY DEAL WITH?
options: 1) It deals with sheets of epithelial cells cultured in vitro.
2) It manufactures synthetic compounds such as silicone.
3) It is largely concerned with the bodily surface.
4) It deals with treating the deep-seated tumors.
correct: 3) It is largely concerned with the bodily surface.

question: ОПРЕДЕЛИТЕ ОСНОВНУЮ ИДЕЮ ТЕКСТА

4) Microsurgery allows surgeons to reattach severed fingers and limbs. Text 4 MORE SPECIALITIES IN MEDICINE
correct: 3) Plastic surgery is concerned with the collection and improvement of severe functional impairments.

question: HEALTH BUDGET OF INSTITUTIONS IS INCREASINGLY SPENT ON SPECIALISATION.

BOTH INCREASE OF KNOWLEDGE IN THE MEDICAL SCIENCES AND ADVANCES IN TECHNOLOGY.
ОТВЕТЬТЕ НА ВОПРОС: WHY IS THE SPECIALIST'S SKILL IN MEDICINE ESPECIALLY IMPORTANT NOWADAYS?
options: 1) It is important because of the appearance of AIDS.
2) It is important because of the introduction of new technologies and equipment.
3) It is important because the number of patients is rapidly growing.
4) It is important because being a physician is a very hard job.
correct: 2) It is important because of the introduction of new technologies and equipment.

question: IRRADIATION IS MORE EFFECTIVE IN TREATING TISSUES THAT ARE WELL OXYGENATED.
INTERACTING WITH TISSUE IONISING RADIATION CAUSES CELL DEATH.
RADIOACTIVE IMPLANTS CAN DELIVER HIGH DOSES OF RADIATION DIRECTLY INTO THE TUMOUR WITH MORE EFFECT ON DISTANT TISSUES.

THE TOLERANCE THRESHOLDS TO RADIATION IS DIFFERENT IN HEALTHY ORGANS.
ОТВЕТЬТЕ НА ВОПРОС: WHAT IS THE ADVANTAGE OF RADIATION THERAPY IN TREATING TUMOURS COMPARED TO OTHER METHODS?

2) It may cause less damage than surgery.
3) It can transmit energy by electromagnetic waves.
4) It can be enhanced with some drugs.
correct: 2) It may cause less damage than surgery.

question: TECHNOLOGICAL ADVANCES MAKE HOSPITAL RUNNING MORE EXPENSIVE.

options: 1) Hospitals are equipped with laboratory facilities.

question: THE FIRST CLINIC IN THE ENGLISH-SPEAKING COUNTRIES WAS FOUNDED IN THE XVIITH CENTURY.
THE PHYSICIANS IN THE CLINICS AND IN THE HOSPITAL ARE USUALLY THE SAME SPECIALISTS.
THE PHYSICIANS OF THE LONDON DISPENSARY WERE TREATING THEIR PATIENTS IN THE CLINIC.
PRACTICALLY ALL CLINICS CHARGE A SMALL REGISTRATION FEE IF THE PATIENT IS ABLE TO PAY.
THE FIRST AMERICAN CLINICS WERE ESTABLISHED IN THE XVIIITH CENTURY.
THE NUMBER OF HOSPITAL CLINICS GREW RAPIDLY BECAUSE OF THE CONVENIENCE OF PROVIDING AMBULATORY CARE CLOSE TO THE FACILITIES OF A HOSPITAL.
ОТВЕТЬТЕ НА ВОПРОС: WHAT DOES THE TERM "CLINIC" MEAN?
options: 1) It denotes a specific subdivision of a hospital for treating outpatients.
2) It is a facility for diagnosis and treatment of inpatients.
3) It is a class of medical institution in which patients are examined and discussed.
4) It means a group meeting devoted to the acquiring of specific skills or knowledge. Text 8 ARMY MEDICAL ORGANIZATION
correct: 1) It denotes a specific subdivision of a hospital for treating outpatients.

question: MEDICAL OFFICERS CAN USE ARMS IF THEIR PATIENTS ARE BEING ATTACKED.
THE COMMANDING OFFICER OF A HOSPITAL MAY HAVE NO CONTACTS WITH PATIENTS.
MEDICAL OFFICERS HAVE TO OBEY THE SUPERIOR OFFICER IN GIVING TREATMENT TO THE PATIENT.
ARMIES AND AIR FORCES MAY SHARE THE SAME MEDICAL SERVICES.

ОТВЕТЬТЕ НА ВОПРОС: WHERE CAN ARMY PHYSICIANS OBTAIN THEIR QUALIFICATIONS?
options: 1) They may obtain their qualifications while being the army.
2) They must obtain their qualifications only in the medical institution. 83
3) The qualifications are usually obtained by army physicians through participation in war actions.
4) They can get practice in different medical units.
correct: 1) They may obtain their qualifications while being the army.

question: ОПРЕДЕЛИТЕ ОСНОВНУЮ ИДЕЮ ТЕКСТА
options: 1) The medical doctor prescribes drugs and dressings.
2) The army medical organization has its special features.

4) In a war zone, medical officers apply first aid to the walking wounded. Text 9 PUBLIC HEALTH CARE
correct: 2) The army medical organization has its special features.

question: HEALTH CONTROL AT AIRPORTS IS A PREVENTIVE MEASURE AGAINST BRINGING INFECTIOUS DISEASES INTO A COUNTRY.

THE ENVIRONMENT IN WHICH PEOPLE WORK IS A CONCERN OF PUBLIC HEALTH MEDICINE.
ОТВЕТЬТЕ НА ВОПРОС: WHAT INFORMATION DO EPIDEMIOLOGISTS PROVIDE?
options: 1) Statistics collected by epidemiologists explain how to put corporate arrangements into practice.
2) They use statistics to provide special diets for the population.
3) They provide data for government through laws and regulations.
4) They collect data and try to explain the relationship between a disease and different environmental and behavioral factors.
correct: 4) They collect data and try to explain the relationship between a disease and different environmental and behavioral factors.

options: 1) Medicine is involved in improving the health, safety, and welfare of population. 85

correct: 2) The art and science of public health deals with preserving, maintaining, and actively promoting physical and mental health of people.

question: BOTH ANIMALS AND PLANTS ARE ALWAYS EXPOSED TO DISEASES.
IT'S MUCH EASIER TO TREAT THE DISEASE WHEN THE CAUSE OF IT IS KNOWN.
POST-OPERATIVE THERAPY MAY INCLUDE SUCH TREATMENT AS CHEMOTHERAPY OR RADIATION THERAPY.

PATHOLOGY DETERMINES STRUCTURAL CHANGES ASSOCIATED WITH THE DISEASE PROCESS.
EFFECTIVE TREATMENT DEPENDS ON THE CONFIDENCE OF A PATIENT IN THE PHYSICIAN AND IN THE METHOD SELECTED.

2) It is necessary for recognizing the symptoms of a disease.

correct: 2) It is necessary for recognizing the symptoms of a disease.
